arts.ttl: Birmingham City set for triple boost but five players could miss Exeter match
arts.sum: Stansfield has missed Blues' last three matches due to a slight issue but manager Chris Davies confirmed the 22-year-old has returned to training pre-Exeter. Tomoki Iwata, Willum Willumsson, Keshi Anderson and Emil Hansson are all struggling with issues.

arts.ttl: Exeter City manager Gary Caldwell given two-match touchline ban
arts.sum: As Caldwell accepted the charge and no details were forthcoming when the commission sought details of Caldwell’s disciplinary record over the past five years, so he was hit with the standard penalty of a one match ban for abuse of the fourth official and £1,000 fine. Whilst not trying to justify his behaviour, Caldwell said his actions were the culmination of those decisions and while he faced two charges of misconduct – one to the fourth official and one to the referee – only a single change should be considered.

arts.ttl: Exeter City suffer blow as Tristan Crama recalled by Brentford
arts.sum: “When you lose a player as good as Tristan, you are disappointed, but I think we’re all happy for him and we’re all proud of the job we’ve done as a football club and as the staff, in terms of welcoming another loan player in and seeing a young player develop and progress in such a short space of time,” Caldwell said. So whilst we’re disappointed - and we have to obviously replace them – the whole football club should be proud and happy for Tristan that he’s getting a really good move and progressing his career.”

arts.ttl: FA Cup: Chelsea, Man Utd, Liverpool and Tottenham new dates revealed as fourth round TV schedule is announced
arts.sum: Some intriguing ties will take place in the fourth round of the FA Cup

arts.ttl: Grant Hanley makes his Birmingham City intentions clear in first interview after transfer
arts.sum: But Hanley's game time has been limited in the past two seasons, particularly this campaign where he has only made four appearances and played 20 minutes of club football since August. Grant Hanley has signed for Birmingham City ‘to play football’ after 18 months of frustration at Championship outfit Norwich City.

arts.ttl: Stansfield returns: Birmingham City team news and predicted XI to face Exeter City
arts.sum: Birmingham City return to League One football this weekend as Exeter City make the trip to St. Andrew’s… The post Stansfield returns: Birmingham City team news and predicted XI to face Exeter City appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: The inside story of Jeremy Dale after the Birmingham City exit that shocked everyone
arts.sum: Cook will remain a member of the club’s Advisory Committee – a group chaired by Tom Brady – and will continue to serve as a trustee of the Birmingham City Foundation. According to his LinkedIn page, Dale is CEO of a company called Punk Rock Business, and has been for the last seven years.

arts.ttl: Birmingham City closing on first January transfer and the latest on Phil Neumann
arts.sum: However, a deal for Hanley would lessen Blues’ chances of signing German centre-back Phil Neumann this month. The 27-year-old is set to sign for Blues on a free transfer next summer.

arts.ttl: Kevin McDonald signs Exeter City contract extension
arts.sum: Despite injuries disrupting his time at St James Park, meaning he has only ever penned short-term contracts since first arriving in Devon last September, the 23-year-old has featured 18 times this season and started four of the last five matches. McDonald is one of three players that are on short-term deals at St James Park and the club have confirmed they are still in talks with the in-form Caleb Watts over extending his stay as well.

arts.ttl: Breaking: Birmingham City CEO Garry Cook steps down for 'personal reasons'
arts.sum: Birmingham City have announced that Garry Cook has stepped down from his position as chief executive due to personal reasons. A club statement said: "The Board has accepted Garry’s decision which he has made for personal reasons but are pleased to confirm that he has agreed to remain a member of the club’s Advisory Committee.
